seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful document retrieval, vector search, and LLM synthesis with built-in tools like file search and code interpreter. However, risks like hallucinated citations, irrelevant context retrieval, and thread bloat can undermine reliability and trust. Vex provides essential verification layers for source faithfulness, hallucination detection, and citation accuracy to ensure production-grade performance.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The OpenAI Assistants API manages RAG pipelines through threads, assistants, and runs. Users upload documents to vector stores, which the assistant's file search tool queries during runs. The code interpreter handles any computation, while function calling enables custom retrieval logic. Context is assembled from retrieved chunks and synthesized by the LLM with source attribution via citations.

Typical flow: 1) Create assistant with file search enabled and vector store attached 2) Start thread and add user message 3) Create run which triggers retrieval, synthesis, and tool calls 4) Poll for completion and extract response with citations. This managed runtime abstracts infrastructure but introduces opaque failure modes around retrieval quality and context limits.

Verification occurs post-run via webhooks, analyzing annotations, citations, and retrieved context against ground truth sources.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate malicious code during data processing or visualization
risks.mitigation: Vex sandbox execution monitoring and taint tracking on code interpreter outputs

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Vector store returns semantically drifted chunks that mislead synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex context relevance scoring with semantic similarity thresholds

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long-running threads accumulate messages exceeding context window without truncation
risks.mitigation: Vex thread length monitoring with automatic summarization triggers

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: LLM generates invalid retrieval parameters leading to empty or wrong results
risks.mitigation: Vex function calling schema validation and parameter hallucination detection

implementation.title: Create RAG Assistant with Vector Store
implementation.description: Initialize assistant with file search enabled and attach uploaded documents to vector store
implementation.code:
import openai
import os

client = openai.OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY'))

# Upload documents
file_streams = [open(f'doc_{i}.pdf', 'rb') for i in range(1, 4)]
file_ids = [client.files.create(file=f, purpose='assistants').id for f in file_streams]

# Create vector store
vector_store = client.beta.vector_stores.create(name='RAG Docs')
client.beta.vector_stores.files.batch_create(
    vector_store_id=vector_store.id, file_ids=file_ids
)

# Create RAG assistant
assistant = client.beta.assistants.create(
    name='RAG Assistant',
    instructions='Answer using only the provided documents. Cite sources.',
    model='gpt-4-turbo-preview',
    tools=[{'type': 'file_search'}],
    tool_resources={'file_search': {'vector_store_ids': [vector_store.id]}}
)
print(f'Assistant ID: {assistant.id}, Vector Store: {vector_store.id}')

implementation.title: Run RAG Query with Citations
implementation.description: Execute query through thread and extract response with source annotations
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

message = client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role='user',
    content='What are the key findings from the Q3 earnings report?'
)

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id
)

# Poll for completion
while run.status in ['queued', 'in_progress']:
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)
    time.sleep(1)

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)
response = messages.data[0].content[0].text
annotations = messages.data[0].content[0].annotations

citations = []
for annotation in annotations:
    if annotation.type == 'file_citation':
        file_citation = annotation.file_citation
        citations.append({
            'text': annotation.text,
            'file_id': file_citation.file_id,
            'quote': file_citation.quote
        })
print('Response:', response.value)
print('Citations:', citations)

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Webhook for Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Register Vex verification webhook on assistant run completion events
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Vex
from openai import OpenAI
import os

vex_client = Vex(api_key=os.getenv('VEX_API_KEY'))
openai_client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY'))

# Create verification suite for RAG
suite = vex_client.suites.create(
    name='OpenAI RAG Verification',
    checks=[
        'source_faithfulness',
        'hallucination_detection',
        'context_relevance',
        'citation_accuracy'
    ]
)

# Register webhook on assistant
assistant = openai_client.beta.assistants.update(
    assistant_id='asst_xxx',
    webhook_url=f'{vex_client.webhook_url}/openai-assistants',
    webhook_secret=os.getenv('VEX_WEBHOOK_SECRET'),
    metadata={'vex_suite_id': suite.id}
)

print(f'Webhook registered. Suite ID: {suite.id}')
